data.title: Sterling Rises Ahead of BOE Decision — Market Talk
data.description:
0736 GMT - Sterling rises following a jump in interest-rate expectations ahead of the Bank of England's decision at 1100 GMT. The market largely expects the BOE to leave rates unchanged Thursday but is pricing 78 basis points of rate rises by year-end following a sharp rise in energy prices due to the Iran war, LSEG data show. That compares to 48 basis points Wednesday. Rate-rise bets look excessive, however, and the BOE's decision could prompt markets to scale back these expectations, ING's Francesco Pesole says in a note. ING expects the BOE to vote 8-1 to leave rates unchanged but doesn't anticipate fresh clues about policy direction. Sterling rises 0.1% to $1.3483. The euro falls 0.1% to 0.8659 pounds. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0718 GMT - The Bank of England is expected to keep interest rates on hold later today despite rising inflation expectations, Kathleen Brooks at XTB says in a note. But as oil prices hit a fresh war-time high of $123 a barrel, the outlook is becoming more uncertain and hikes look more likely, she says. "The BOE may find it hard to avoid straying into hawkish territory," Brooks says. The central bank will emphasize a focus on second-round inflation effects. And a more divided vote, such as a tight 6-3 split toward holding rates, could signal a hike as early as June. "The market is expecting the BOE to signal that rates will remain higher for longer, and for now, U.K. inflation is expected to peak at 4% this year," Brooks says. (don.forbes@wsj.com)

0711 GMT - The Indian rupee has been weighed down by the Middle East conflict, Commerzbank Research analysts say in a research report. The rupee is affected disproportionately more than other Asian currencies because of its sensitivity to oil prices and capital outflows, the analysts say. Also, "higher oil prices have reignited fears of a larger import bill and wider current account deficit" in India, the analysts say. "We look for consolidation in USD-INR in the near term with RBI intervention to contain the upside," the analysts add. The dollar rises 0.4% to 95.2654 rupees, FactSet data show. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0711 GMT - Consumer inflation in the Tokyo metropolitan area is expected to have risen 1.8% in April from a year earlier, according to a poll of economists by data provider Quick. That compares with March's 1.7% rise. Both upward and downward pressures likely played out in a tug-of-war this month. Japanese companies raised prices for their goods and services at the start of the fiscal year in April, while government energy subsidies capped the rise in gasoline prices caused by the Middle East conflict. Tokyo's CPI data is scheduled for release Friday.(megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

0707 GMT - China's economy still shows an uneven growth path, according to Citi analysts in a research note. China's April PMI data suggests growth momentum is extending into 2Q, but though "the headline picture remained broadly solid, the economy's K-shaped divergence has become entrenched," they say. Exports and production remain the key drivers but weakness in domestic orders, construction and services is concerning, they say. Citi maintains its 2026 GDP forecast at 4.7% and remains cautious on domestic demand. Investors will closely watch the U.S.-China summit in mid-May, Citi says, and sees the mid-year China Politburo meeting as "a venue for policy recalibration, with catalysts, if any, on labor market, consumption, and social stability."(tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0653 GMT - Although Middle East tensions have cooled Japanese consumer sentiment, there has been no real damage to private spending so far. Data earlier shows that overall retail sales rose 1.7% in March from the prior year. "Still, with no end in sight to the Middle East conflict and oil prices holding firm at high levels, the risk of a slump in private consumption remains a lingering concern," SMBC Nikko Securities economists say. They look back to the energy cost spike of 2022--which worsened Japan's terms of trade and contributed to a decline in real wages. "If oil prices remain elevated [this time], a similar worsening in the terms of trade could once again undercut real wage growth." (megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

0651 GMT - Eurozone government bond yields jump in opening trade, with the 10-year Bund yield hitting its highest level since 2011, as oil prices rise sharply due to the continued U.S. blockade of Iran in the Strait of Hormuz. The day's immediate focus in the eurozone is the European Central Bank's policy decision. The ECB is widely expected to keep interest rates on hold but investors will seek clues on how the central bank will respond to both the inflationary and growth impact of high oil prices. Eurozone money markets fully price a 25 basis-point rate increase in June and a further two hikes by year-end. The 10-year German Bund yield rises to a high of 3.146%, Tradeweb data show. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0649 GMT - The dollar rises as oil prices jump on concerns about an escalation in the Iran war and after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell confirmed he would stay on the board once his chairmanship ends in May. An Axios report said the U.S. is preparing military strikes to break the negotiation deadlock with Iran. Powell's decision to remain as a governor after his term as chair ends stops Trump from nominating a replacement who favors interest-rate cuts, Danske Bank analysts say in a note. Powell's announcement came as the Fed held rates steady and the U.S. Senate banking committee approved Kevin Warsh's nomination as the next Fed chair. The DXY dollar index rises 0.1% to 99.038. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0621 GMT - Japan's industrial production is expected to decline in the April-June quarter, given that prospects for a resolution to the Middle East conflict remain unclear and crude oil supply is unlikely to recover immediately, SMBC Nikko Securities economists say. While Japan has sufficient oil reserves and has procured alternative sources, supply constraints will likely ripple through third countries, they say. "Close attention must be paid to the possibility of production disruptions spreading via supply chain bottlenecks for products that rely on imports from Asian nations with high Middle East energy dependency." Industrial output fell 0.5% in March from the previous month, government data showed Thursday.(megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

0602 GMT - Gross government bond issuance in the eurozone is expected to rise modestly to 147 billion euros in May, up by about 5 billion euros on month, Barclays's rates strategists say in a note. This anticipated bond supply includes about 31 billion euros in syndicated issuance from Germany, Belgium, Greece, Spain and Portugal, they say. Redemptions will amount to around 90 billion euros in May, down from around 156 billion euros in April, they forecast. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0555 GMT - The only possibility of the yen's weakening trend reversing is if President Trump relaxes his stance on Iran, such as by easing conditions for nuclear talks, says NLI Research Institute economist Tsuyoshi Ueno. The Bank of Japan's further interest-rate increases are unlikely to bolster the yen significantly because the market has already priced in the possibility of a hike in June or July, he says. "Because the BOJ's policy decisions depend on the Middle East situation, officials are unlikely to say anything definitive on the future rate-hike path" before their next meeting in June, he adds. The yen briefly weakened to near 160.60 during afternoon trading. (megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

0554 GMT - The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield stays near a one-month high of 4.434% reached Wednesday following the Federal Reserve's policy meeting, while a renewed rise in oil prices add to inflation concerns. Brent oil is up 4.54% at $123.39, having already increased on Wednesday, after President Trump instructed aides to prepare for an extended blockade of Iran. The Fed left policy rates on hold at 3.50%-3.75%, but in a surprising vote split with four members dissenting. The two-year U.S. Treasury yield rises 0.9 basis point to 3.939% and the 10-year yield rises 1.4 basis points to 4.429%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: MTU Aero Engines Sees Solid Start to the Year — Market Talk
data.description: MTU Aero Engines shows a solid start to the year, with the strong free cash flow the key positive highlight, RBC Capital Markets analyst Mark Fielding says in a research note. The German aircraft-engine manufacturer has an attractive exposure to the civil aerospace and defense segment, as well as the maintenance, repair and overhaul activity, Fielding says. "However, we see the share as relatively fairly valued vs peers," the analyst says. Shares trade 0.9% lower at 281 euros. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

data.title: Samsung Heavy Industries Q1 Profit Rises On Sales Growth
data.description:
SEOUL (dpa-AFX) - Samsung Heavy Industries Co., Ltd. (SMSHF, 010140.KS) reported Thursday higher profit and sales for its first quarter.

In the first quarter, net income attributable to shareholders of parent company grew 10.3 percent to 101.6 billion Korean won from last year's 92.1 billion won.

Operating income surged 122 percent to 273.1 billion won from 123.1 billion won last year.

Sales climbed 16.4 percent to 2.90 trillion won from 2.49 trillion won a year ago.

In South Korea, th shares closed Thursday's regular trading at 32,350.00 won, down 2 percent.

data.title: Salmon Evolution ASA: New share capital registered
data.description:
30 April 2026: Reference is made to the stock exchange announcement published by Salmon Evolution ASA (the “Company”) on 29 April 2026 regarding a successfully placed private placement (the “Private Placement”) and a retail offering (the “Retail Offering”) as described therein. Reference is also made to the resolution by the Board of Directors to issue 89 million new shares in the Company in the Private Placement and 2.4 million new shares in the Company in the Retail Offering, both at a subscription price of NOK 4.50 per share, pursuant to an authorisation granted by the Company’s general meeting on 5 June 2025.

The share capital increases pertaining to the Private Placement and the Retail Offering have today been registered with the Norwegian Register of Business Enterprises (Nw. Foretaksregisteret) and the Norwegian Central Securities Depository (Euronext Securities Oslo or the “VPS”). Following the registration, the Company's new share capital is NOK 27,700,165.30, divided into 554,003,306 ordinary shares, each with a nominal value of NOK 0.05.

For further information, please contact:

Trond Håkon Schaug-Pettersen, CEO: +47 911 91 327

Trond Vadset Veibust, CFO: +47 480 90 595

This information is subject to the disclosure requirements pursuant to section 5-12 of the Norwegian Securities Trading Act.

About Salmon Evolution

Salmon Evolution is the global leader within land-based salmon farming. Pioneering the hybrid flow-through system (HFS), Salmon Evolution is Extending the Ocean Potential by creating optimal growth conditions in a controlled environment on land. This approach, capturing the benefits of both land-based and sea-based farming, puts biology first and limits operational and biological risk.

Salmon Evolution is strategically located at the heart of the global aquaculture industry on the west coast of Norway, where the Company has its first facility and global centre of excellence fully operational at industrial scale. Enabled by proof of concept at Indre Harøy, Salmon Evolution targets significant expansion.

Salmon Evolution is listed on Oslo Børs under the ticker SALME. To learn more, please visit www.salmonevolution.no.

Attachments

Salmon Evolution ASA: New share capital registered

data.title: Ghitha Holding records strong Q1 revenue of $441mln
data.description:
Staff Writer

ABU DHABI - Ghitha Holding, a subsidiary of 2PointZero, has announced its consolidated financial results for the three-month period ended 31st March 2026.

During the period, revenue grew by 23 percent YoY to AED1.62 billion, driven by strong momentum across the Group’s core business segments. Performance was supported primarily by organic growth across key categories, and continued integration, reflecting the resilience of Ghitha’s diversified platform and sustained demand across essential food segments.

Gross profit rose 8 percent YoY to AED330.8 million on the back of higher revenues, and ongoing optimisation across operations.

Falal Ameen, CEO of Ghitha Holding, said, “Ghitha delivered a strong start to 2026, with double-digit revenue growth reflecting the resilience of our platform and strong demand across our core categories. Our performance was driven by the strength of our integrated operating model and our ability to deliver consistent growth amid a challenging geopolitical environment.

We continue to play a central role in supporting the UAE’s food security agenda, ensuring reliable supply of essential food products. At the same time, the acquisiton of Taaza by NRTC strengthens our presence in the fruits and vegetables segment and enhances our farm-to-market capabilities. Looking ahead, we remain focused on driving operational efficiencies and pursuing disciplined expansion opportunities that deliver sustainable value and attractive returns.”

During the quarter, Ghitha continued to strengthen its role in supporting national food security, leveraging its integrated platform to meet local demand and ensure continuity of supply across essential food categories.

The Group will continue the integration and expansion of Taaza under NRTC, scaling operations and distribution capabilities to support growth in the fresh-cut, juice, and adjacent product segments. This reinforces Ghitha’s strategy to deepen its presence in high-demand categories and enhance vertical integration across the fresh produce value chain.

At the operational level, Ghitha maintained progress on the implementation of SAP, a strategic initiative aimed at enhancing efficiency, integration, and scalability across the platform.

Looking ahead, the Group remains focused on disciplined growth across its core segments, pursuing opportunities that meet its return thresholds while continuing to drive operational efficiencies and scale benefits across the business.

Ghitha is well-positioned to sustain its growth trajectory while contributing meaningfully to the UAE’s long-term food security objectives.

Article Source: Emirates News Agency (WAM)

data.title: U.S. Futures Down, European Stocks Mixed
data.description:
U.S. futures for the S&P 500 were down 0.2% and futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.6%. Changes in futures do not necessarily predict movements after the opening bell.

In Europe, the Stoxx Europe 600 declined 0.5% in morning trading. ARCADIS added 13.3% and United Utilities Group rose 10.2%. On the other hand, Technip Energies dropped 8.8%, and Stellantis lost 7.5%. The FTSE 100 was flat. Other stocks in Europe were down as France's CAC 40 decreased 1.1% and Germany's DAX fell 0.6%.

The Wall Street Journal Dollar Index was flat at 95.85.

In commodities, Brent crude climbed 1.8% to $112.43 a barrel, and WTI crude gained 1.3% to $108.29 a barrel. The European benchmark price for natural gas, the Dutch futures contract TTF, was up 0.9% to 47.3 euros a megawatt hour.

The German 10-year Bund yield rose by 1 basis point to 3.122% from 3.111%, and the yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury fell by 1 basis point to 4.424% from 4.434%. Bond prices and yields move in opposite directions.

Stocks in Asia were mixed as Japan's Nikkei 225 index dropped 1.1%, and Hong Kong's Hang Seng declined 1.1%, whereas China's benchmark Shanghai Composite climbed 0.1%.

-This article was automatically generated by Dow Jones Newswires.

Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: Wilmar's Refining Margins Could Remain Under Pressure — Market Talk
data.description: Wilmar International's refining margins could remain under pressure, says DBS Group Research's William Simadiputra in a note, citing crude palm oil's strong price trend. The commodities market volatility also poses a key risk to the Singapore-listed agribusiness's earnings performance, he says. Still, he keeps his earnings projections for the company unchanged for now, as losses from hedging activities are expected to reverse in the coming quarters when physical transactions are settled. DBS maintains its hold rating and S$3.60 target price. Shares are down 8.9% at S$3.49 after earlier dropping as much as 10%.(megan.cheah@wsj.com)

data.title: Centurion Corp. Set to Benefit From Asset Acquisitions in Western Australia — Market Talk
data.description: Centurion Corp. stands to benefit from its acquisition of two assets in Western Australia, CGS International analysts say in a research report. Its acquisition of worker-accommodation assets in Karratha and in South Hedland mark a strategic pivot into a higher-yielding market, the analysts say. With around 90% financial occupancy supported by mostly corporate leases and net-property-income margins of roughly 70%, the assets are expected to generate low double-digit yields, the analysts estimate. Beyond earnings accretion, these acquisitions establish a strategic beachhead in Western Australia. The brokerage raises the stock's target price to S$1.89 from S$1.84 with an unchanged add rating. Shares are unchanged at S$1.63. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: OWLS: Transition year with infrastructure buildout, Nasdaq listing, and set-up for 2026 enterprise growth
data.description:
2025 marked a foundational year with Nasdaq listing, core infrastructure buildout, and expanded regulatory coverage. Revenue grew modestly to $7.9M, while net loss widened due to non-cash and one-time listing costs. OwlPay Harbor is positioned for enterprise-scale growth in 2026.

data.title: Synthomer lifts prices to offset Iran war costs, benefits from volume gains
data.description:
Specialty chemicals supplier Synthomer said on Thursday it has offset higher costs linked to the Iran war through "substantial" pricing action and higher volumes, supporting trading in a volatile environment.

Shares in the company soared 27% to 63.7 pence. Synthomer did not disclose by how much it raised prices.

Since the start of the Iran war, higher raw material and energy costs have been passed on through pricing, while shipment volumes have risen in several base chemical products due to disruption at competitors, particularly in Asia, the company said.

First-quarter trading was in line with estimates and higher year-on-year, Synthomer said, forecasting a strong quarter-on-quarter volume and margin growth for the next quarter, supported by pricing measures taken after Iran war disrupted operating environment.

The company added that its global supply chains have so far remained resilient, with its joint venture manufacturing operation and sales office in the Middle East continuing to function normally.

Operating across coatings, construction, health and protection, and adhesives, Synthomer kept its 2026 outlook unchanged, saying it still expects year-on-year progress driven by cost-cutting efforts and margin gains in its speciality businesses.

Continuing underlying operating profit fell 21.8% to 37.6 million pounds ($50.7 million) for the year ended December 31.

($1 = 0.7417 pounds)

data.title: Amrize AG reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Amrize AG reported a quarterly adjusted loss of 21 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31. The mean expectation of eight analysts for the quarter was for a loss of 13 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from -19 cents to -10 cents per share.

  • Revenue was $2.18 billion​; analysts expected $2.14 billion.

  • Amrize AG's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of 21 cents​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $116 million.

  • Amrize AG shares had risen by 2.1% this quarter and gained 5.8% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 14.3% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, two analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 12 "strong buy" or "buy," 8 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the construction materials peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Amrize AG is $69.00, about 17.7% above its last closing price of $56.77

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data April 30 at 07:30 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Spain's economic growth decelerates to 0.6% in first quarter, still solid
data.description:
Spain's economic growth slowed down 0.6% in the first quarter after a 0.8% expansion in the previous three-month period, slightly exceeding expectations as Spain continued to lead major economies in Europe.

On an annual basis, gross domestic product expanded 2.7% in the quarter, flash data released by the National Statistics Institute (INE) showed on Thursday. The INE revised fourth-quarter GDP growth downward - to 2.6% from 2.7%.

Analysts polled by LSEG had forecast GDP to grow 0.5% quarter-on-quarter and 2.7% year-on-year.

"At this pace, we continue to lead among the major economies of the European Union," Economy Minister Carlos Cuerpo said in a video released by his team on Thursday.

Cuerpo said growth was led by household consumption and investment, adding that productivity metrics also improved in the first quarter.

External demand detracted 0.7 percentage points from the annual GDP change, the INE said.

The Spanish government still expects the economy to grow 2.2% this year, although Cuerpo said earlier this week that uncertainty triggered by the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran could weigh on the forecast.

The government's GDP prediction could alter by 0.1 to 0.8 percentage points depending on the evolution of fluctuating oil prices, according to government and central bank projections, he said.
